keep.reason: Moved up as the clearest applied skim hook: for complete-data-equivalent AIC/BIC selection, add the per-candidate trace because uncorrected tables tilt toward high-missing-information models.
Kept high for boundary guidance: use centering where the statistic is used directly, but do not stack it on a Chan-calibrated null MI LRT.
Kept as the first-class workflow contribution, scoped to this study's documented provenance and gates rather than a general guarantee.
Kept as the workflow's applied implication: it translates this study's audit gates into concrete standards teams can demand of AI-assisted derivations.
Kept as the foundational theorem, with the necessary congenial proper-MI, estimated-scale, complete-data-MLE, and MCAR/MAR scoping.
Kept because it supplies the theoretical IC centering term and model-specificity behind the practical AIC/BIC correction.
Kept because it prevents the common LRT mistake of using the naive per-model trace difference instead of the projected tested-direction trace.
Added from the cut list as a distinct actionable LRT implementation point, scoped to the proved null and local-alternative Monte Carlo noise reduction.

drop_notes: Omitted K17 as redundant and less scoped than K09; omitted K04/K13/K07/K08 because K10/K03/K11 cover their main guardrails without duplicating the final set.
